title: [NO TESTS NEEDED] Update cirrus vms for testing
body:
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@cevich is this the correct VM to check?
@cevich @mheon @vrothberg Just updating the repo is causing all of the errors.

Ya that's the right image ID.  The [APIv2 failure is the flake we've seen](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5501586081316864?command=main#L687) in a few of the daily cirrus-cron runs.  That's issue https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9328

[The `podman run blkio-weight test` failure](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4586792407007232/html/int-podman-fedora-33-root-host.log.html#t--podman-run-blkio-weight-test--1) I brought to @giuseppe notice a few weeks ago, IIRC, it looks familiar.  I swore I opened an issue on this...but apparently not :disappointed:  (or I couldn't find it).  It's a real problem (somewhere, kernel update?), I remember going onto a VM and was able to reproduce it.  I also verified the cgroups 1 vs 2 range-conversion on "15" math is correct (it should spit out "51"). I want to say Giuseppe reported it fixed by a crun update, but can't be sure.  However, clearly something is missing or disconnected somewhere.

[The "podman network with multiple aliases" is new to me](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4586792407007232/html/int-podman-fedora-33-root-host.log.html#t--podman-network-with-multiple-aliases--1), I don't recall seeing that before.

Have you tried `hack/get_ci_vm.sh`?

Yes We have a simple fix for the 51->15 and fixed.

The Multiple networks, I am not sure what is going on there.
The API Test failing, I am also confused, but the podman in podman test failures are the most curios.

> the podman in podman test failures are the most curios.

Meaning the `int podman fedora-33 root container` task:
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5431217337139200?command=main#L6021

I'm confused :confused:  Those (in this PR) look like the same failures we see on the VMs?
